pages.full_text:
Monday 31st May.
		Finally it has stopped raining. Peter flew his kite. William + John climbed in the rocks. Eve got William to take a family portrait from the A frame [?stage] to the rocks.
		We set off in the van + walked in Cwm Nantcol, a superb gorge with waterfall. Simon held Charles' hand. John + Thomas were dangerous. We read about erratic boulders.
		In the afternoon we drove high up through solitary farm buildings + finally walked to + from the __Door of Ardudwy__, 3 miles altogether, now a boggy track, but once an important road from the coast into central Wales.
		Back at the campsite it is windy, Peter is flying the kite. We are about to have a barbeque.
		Simon is laughing at Charles talking all the time, even to himself. At lunch Simon drove into a bolder + smashed a bit of the numberplate.
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-left corner. Date is 'Monday 31st May' — in the 1989-2003 range, May 31st fell on a Monday in 1993 and 1999; year cannot be determined from page content alone so iso_guess left null. 'A frame [?stage]' — word between 'frame' and 'to' is unclear, possibly 'stage' or 'side'. 'bolder' is the diarist's spelling of 'boulder'.

pages.full_text:
__Menu at Sinas__

[glued-in paper slip:]
Fri l  picnic [?chicken]
    S  [?Tuna] [?Riche]    Strawb. cr.
         carrots,
Sat l  ham + rolls  Olives    apples
    S  Tongue + salad      ap + al sp
Sun l  bacon sandw.    cherries [?shortd]
    S  [?Riche] nuts [?peanutbut]  Bakewell tart
          [?cel-ery] [?mam crch]
Mon l  sausage + rolls    kiwi  [?charlse]
    S  [?artichoke hearts]
       tuna + salad       car flan    — barbeque
                                       instead
Tues l  cheese + toms    prunes
         + beans/nuts
     s  tinned ham        treacle tart

tea  Auntie Olive, rock cakes,
               [?flap] [?jacks]
]

We had a lovely Barbeque + then sat by the campfire, Simon, Thom[as] + Charles [h]ad made a fire, instead of the normal box barbeque.
Tuesday  Again it poured rain all day. We drove home via Powys Castle, which turned out to be closed, + __Montgomery__,
pages.notes: No handwritten page number visible in top-right corner. Right page contains a glued-in paper slip with a meal menu headed 'Menu at Sinas' (possibly a campsite name). The menu slip is partially obscured by tape/adhesive residue, making several items hard to read — marked with [?] throughout. 'Sinas' could be a campsite name in Wales; possibly 'Sinas' or '[?Sinai]' but 'Sinas' is the clearest reading. Menu columns appear to be: day, L (lunch) / S (supper), food items. 'Tues' line is cut off at page edge — text continues '+ Montgomery,' suggesting more text on a following page. 'Thom' is cut off at gutter — likely 'Thomas'.
